pattern: Advance a fast pointer two steps for every one step of a slow pointer; when the fast pointer runs off the end, the slow pointer sits on the middle, leaning to the later node when the count is even.
hints: You can find the midpoint without first measuring the length if you let two cursors move at different speeds.
Step one cursor by one node and another by two nodes on each iteration; the slow cursor reaches the centre exactly as the fast cursor reaches the end.
Loop while the fast cursor and its next link both exist, moving slow by one and fast by two; return the slow cursor, which naturally lands on the second of two centres for even lengths.

edge_inputs.description: odd length lands on the single centre
even length must return the later of the two centres
repeated values do not confuse the pointer race

stress.description: longer random list within the value and length bounds
stress.note: exercises both parity outcomes across many lengths
